channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This agency is tasked with protecting elections from cyber attacks. If Trump wins, it could be in danger
item.description: Jen Easterly’s confirmation by the Senate in July 2021 to become director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) was about as smooth as Senate votes get. With a résumé that includes time in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: HHS pushes for artificial intelligence and behavioral health data
item.description: "This program addresses fast emerging challenges that inhibit the development use and advancement of interoperable Health IT," said Alison Kemp. The post HHS pushes for artificial intelligence and behavioral health data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: How to examine files on Linux
item.description: There are many types of files on Linux systems and numerous ways to look at them, from listing permissions to viewing content. You just need to pick the right command to get the information you need.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How IT leaders can assess “secure-by-design” software claims
item.description: It’s no secret cyberthreats are increasing. The cost of reported cybercrime in the US jumped 22% last year to more than $12.5 billion, according to the FBI’s Internet Crime Complaint Center.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How CIOs and CISOs can partner to improve workforce productivity and security
item.description: Many organizations are still struggling to adjust to flexible work requirements, while maintaining enterprise cybersecurity. One key reason is that CIOs and chief information security officers (CISOs) are not always aligned in how to enable productive, secure work.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Faces UK Competition Probe Over Anthropic Partnership
item.description: Google's partnership with artificial intelligence startup Anthropic under scrutiny by the UK's competition watchdog
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Nigeria Drops Charges Against Binance Executive Over Health Concerns
item.description: Nigerian authorities release Tigran Gambaryan on health and diplomatic grounds, ending his money laundering trial
item.dc:creator: Chinedu Asadu And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: iOS 18.1 will fix a bug that made it harder to free up iPhone storage
item.description: With many-megapixel cameras and the ability to shoot video at up to 4K, an iPhone’s storage fills up quickly. Sure, ballooning apps and digital downloads like audiobooks and movies also take up space, but they are easy to offload and reload as needed.
item.dc:creator: Sean Captain, Fast Company

item.title: Woman says teen son died by suicide amid relationship with AI chatbot
item.description: Sewell Setzer III, died by suicide after a monthslong, “hypersexualized” relationship with an AI character, his mother said in a federal lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: AI is now a crucial tool in Trump’s image-management arsenal
item.description: An image of Donald Trump cosplaying as a football player last weekend set the tone for the final push of the Republican nominee’s campaign. It depicts Trump as a yoked, veiny-arm baller glowering from within a Steelers uniform on the field at Pittsburgh’s Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Oxford Generative AI Summit Highlights Need for Nuanced Regulation
item.description: Generative AI summit at University of Oxford sparks debate on regulation, focusing on real harms and flexibility to prevent negative consequences while allowing positive aspects to flourish.
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Musk voter lottery might violate federal law: DOJ
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has warned Elon Musk’s pro-Trump super PAC that its daily $1 million giveaways to registered voters in swing states might violate federal law, according to several news outlets.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Not your father’s avatar: The real future of artificial intelligence
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence (genAI) is the latest milestone in the “AAA” journey, which began with the automation of the mundane, lead to augmentation — mostly machine-driven but lately also expanding into human augmentation — and has built up to artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ask the CIO: HHS Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response
item.description: Learn how critical infrastructure providers can enhance their cybersecurity preparedness and resilience. The post Ask the CIO: HHS Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: ServiceNow, Rimini Street partner for a new ERP cost-saving model
item.description: ServiceNow and end‑to‑end enterprise software support provider Rimini Street have jointly developed a new enterprise software model, designed to reduce costs for enterprises by providing an alternative to digitally transforming existing ERP software.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Benchmarks without context are meaningless: The pitfalls of data without insight
item.description: It was a typical conversation with a client when the question came up: “What’s the average security spend for organizations like ours?” I’ve heard variations of this query countless times.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russia amplified hurricane disinformation to drive Americans apart, researchers find
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia has helped amplify and spread false and misleading internet claims about recent hurricanes in the United States and the federal government's response, part of a wider effort by the Kremlin to manipulate America's political discourse before the presidential election, new rese
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges
item.description: "A lot of the efforts that we're doing across the government have a plug into the artificial intelligence world," said Andy Kilianski. The post Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Hydrogen startup Verdagy opens San Jose plant at risky moment
item.description: The Silicon Valley company is firing up its electrolyzer factory even as hydrogen demand has been slow to grow.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: As Romania prepares to phase out coal by 2032, some miners are retraining for jobs in renewable energies.
item.dc:creator: Anca Gurzu, Cipher News, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meet Capacities, the do-it-all productivity app taking on Notion
item.description: Here’s a tantalizing little paradox to chew over: One of the most interesting up-and-coming productivity apps is also one of the most immediately familiar-feeling—on the surface, at least. The app is called Capacities.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Hong Kong discovers dinosaur fossils for the first time
item.description: Officials in Hong Kong say they have discovered dinosaur fossils in the city for the first time on a remote, uninhabited island that’s part of a geopark.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why it’s so challenging to deploy AI on space missions
item.description: Everything is more difficult in space, and learning software is no exception. But the pressure to push AI into everything can’t be denied. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Crypto's impact on 2024 election will be 'fascinating to see': Mulvaney
item.description: NewsNation political contributor Mick Mulvaney said crypto donations in upcoming elections will be "fascinating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, The Hill

item.title: Developers embracing API-first development, survey says
item.description: Developers are shifting from code-first to API-first thinking when it comes to software development, according to the newly released 2024 State of the API Report from API platform provider Postman.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Jet-tracker who watched Zuckerberg, Trump, Musk says Meta has banned his accounts
item.description: Meta has removed accounts that track the plane movements of its CEO Mark Zuckerberg, X owner Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, Kim Kardashian, former President Trump and others, account owner Jack Sweeney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here’s how to stay safe from scams
item.description: As scammers find new ways to steal money and personal information, consumers should be more vigilant about who they trust, especially online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: A senior U.S. intelligence official says viral online content spreading baseless attacks on Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz was created by Russia.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Historic Alliance: AMD and Intel Join Forces to Counter Arm's Rise - "The Enemy of My Enemy is My Ally"
item.description: AMD, Intel, and industry partners form the x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group to drive forward the ecosystem around x86 processors amidst growing competition from Arm-based solutions.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Michigan secretary of state pushes back against Musk's voter registration claims
item.description: Michigan Secretary of State Jocelyn Benson (D) and tech billionaire Elon Musk got into a days-long back-and-forth on the social platform X about voter registration in the Wolverine State.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: State Department making sure change is more than just a name
item.description: Kelly Fletcher, the State Department’s CIO, said the newly named Bureau of Diplomatic Technology is aligning cybersecurity and customer experience. The post State Department making sure change is more than just a name first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5
